summary: src/drift.rs's entry_shape_of hand-reparses the collection-address wire label kind::collection_address_from_row already resolves loud; wire the emit call site to the shared function instead

edit.description: `entry_shape_of` (1599-1645) hand-parses the `object`/`scalar(field)`/`group-array(member_key;fields)` wire label plus a hooks/enabledPlugins fallback — the exact job `crate::kind::entry_shape_from_label` (reached through the already-imported `collection_address_from_row`, `use crate::kind::{..., collection_address_from_row}` at line 33) already performs through the vocab-checked path. The doc comment at 1601-1602 admits it 'Mirrors the same fallback logic [`collection_address_from_row`] applies'. Its sole call site (1143-1150) already holds `facts: &KindFactRow` and can call `collection_address_from_row(facts)?.map(|addr| addr.entry_shape)` instead — the identical pattern this same file already uses at line 1754 inside `manifest_segment_reaps` on the same `KindFactRow` shape. Delete `entry_shape_of` (1599-1645) once the call site is rewired. `collection_key_of` (1595-1597) is untouched — it resolves a different fact (a registration's manifest segment key off `RegistrationRow::key_path`, still needed at line 1135) that `collection_address_from_row` does not compute. The consolidation also tightens behavior: `entry_shape_of` silently falls back to `EntryShape::Object` on a malformed `group-array(...)` or an unrecognized label (1624-1631), where the shared path rejects the identical malformed input loud via `LockRowError::Vocabulary` — already unit-tested (`src/kind.rs` ~1608/1634/1787; `tests/lock_declaration_rows.rs:1399,1416`).

tests.asserts: a_synthesized_tap_hook_projects_its_matcher_group_through_the_registration_loop and a_represented_manifest_emits_whole_through_the_write_face_not_json_splice still resolve GroupArray/Object entry shapes identically through the registration loop after the consolidation

acceptance: The registration loop's entry-shape resolution in src/drift.rs runs through `collection_address_from_row` alone; `entry_shape_of` no longer exists in the file. `cargo test` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
notes: collection_key_of is not touched — it computes a different fact (a registration's manifest segment key) with no shared-function precedent to fold onto. Scoped at 721cab6.

summary: src/drift.rs's config_stale reimplements config_stale_from_doc's loop verbatim instead of parsing once and delegating, unlike every other path/doc sibling pair in the same file

edit.description: `config_stale` (2642-2669) reimplements `config_stale_from_doc`'s (2674-2704) whole loop body verbatim — same field destructure, same read-and-hash comparison, same `Diagnostic::warn` construction — instead of parsing the lock once and delegating. Every other path/doc sibling pair in this same file already delegates: `walk_lock_rows` (2193-2196) calls `walk_lock_rows_from_doc`, `source_deps` (2807-2823) calls `source_deps_from_doc`, `source_dep_stale` (2919-2941) calls `source_dep_stale_from_doc`. Rewrite `config_stale` to call `read_lock_document_for_emit(workspace_dir)` (the same parse `walk_lock_rows` already performs) then `config_stale_from_doc(&doc, workspace_dir)`, deleting the duplicated loop (2643-2668 collapses to the delegation).

tests.asserts: the existing config_stale assertions (lines 539, 613) still pass unchanged once config_stale delegates instead of reimplementing

acceptance: `config_stale` contains no comparison loop of its own — it parses the lock once and calls `config_stale_from_doc`. `cargo test` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
notes: Same cost profile either way — walk_lock_rows-equivalent parsing already does one read plus one parse — so this is a duplication fix, not a cost-hoist fix. Scoped at 721cab6.

summary: src/drift.rs's write_rollup carries a dead custom rollup-map parameter and loop — its sole call site already merges every kind into one map and passes an empty placeholder for custom

per.section: A shared concept is one type

edit.description: `write_rollup`'s `custom: &BTreeMap<String, Vec<RollupEntry>>` parameter (485) and its write loop (494-496) are dead: `emit`'s own rollup construction (1437-1467) already accumulates every kind — built-in and custom-declared alike — into one `rollups: BTreeMap<String, Vec<RollupEntry>>` keyed by `projection.kind`, and the sole call site (1501-1508) passes that one map as `builtins` and always passes `&BTreeMap::new()` as `custom`. `write_rollup` is `pub(crate)` with `emit` as its only caller (no other call site in `src/`/`tests/`), so the parameter can never receive a non-empty map in production. Collapse the signature to one rollup-entries map parameter, drop the dead loop, and update the doc comment (465-471) which still narrates 'the built-in kinds first (key-sorted) then the custom kinds (name-sorted)' — a two-partition story the data no longer carries.

tests.asserts: lock.toml roll-up output is byte-unchanged (kind rows still key-sorted, name-sorted within a kind) after write_rollup collapses to one map parameter

acceptance: `write_rollup` takes one rollup-entries map; no call site constructs an empty placeholder map to satisfy a second parameter. `cargo test` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
notes: Likely residue from the retired custom/builtin kind split (open-questions.md's 'kinds/ + packages/ curated trees — RETIRED' record); this entry only prunes the dead parameter, it doesn't chase that history further. Scoped at 721cab6.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (75) sits under a doc (71-74) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (650) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (677), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 548 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 647-648 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 672-675 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). f6731fb (DIRECTIVE_FIELD consolidated into graph.rs ahead of this region, in the d1af9a5..HEAD window) shifted every address below its insertion point; re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff), so every address above now carries at its new line.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1547) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1548-1552) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1550) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. Addresses re-read and confirmed at HEAD (0522eff) after upstream graph.rs/test-file shifts; they carry at these lines.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at ac40c72 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. f6731fb shifted graph.rs's const region from 59/55-58 to 75/71-74 (DIRECTIVE_FIELD moved in ahead of it); value and cite date unchanged (5, 2026-07-02). `files[]` line cites rewritten to match. The park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 0522eff.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at ac40c72, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff a23269d..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
